title: DNS lookup slow for public names
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

DNS resolution is slow from inside a container that uses a container network. The local DNS takes ~5s to fail and then the DNS falls back to using the backup DNS configured in resolv.conf

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman network create testnetwork`

2. `podman run -it --name testcontainer alpine sh`

3. `podman network connect testnetwork testcontainer`

4. `time nslookup google.com`

**Describe the results you received:**

nslookup takes 5s to receive the dns entry

```
# time nslookup -debug google.com
Server:         8.8.8.8
Address:        8.8.8.8:53

Query #0 completed in 192ms:
Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 142.251.33.206

Query #1 completed in 193ms:
Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 2607:f8b0:4004:837::200e

real    0m 5.25s
user    0m 0.00s
sys     0m 0.02s
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

nslookup finishes in <1s

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The issue is persistent for me in my buildroot/embedded setup. I did not try to reproduce it yet on another OS though.
The issue happens in a debian based container too.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

OS is running on buildroot/busybox.

comments.body:
If this is just some configuration mistake from my side, I'm sorry about that.
Thanks for any help in advance!

I really enjoy podman, thanks for all the great work!

Hi @Nilos Thanks for creating the issue. I'm unable to reproduce this on both rootless and rootfull on my host. Could you make sure your host resolver does not contains any bad resolver cause fallback dns will try all resolvers in order.

```console
$ sudo podman run -it --rm --name testcontainer --network testnetwork alpine sh
WARN[0000] Ignoring global metacopy option, not supported with booted kernel 
/ # time nslookup google.com
Server:		10.89.2.1
Address:	10.89.2.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:	google.com
Address: 142.250.195.142

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:	google.com
Address: 2404:6800:4007:820::200e

real	0m 0.01s
user	0m 0.00s
sys	0m 0.00s

```

How does `/etc/resolv.conf` look in the container and on your host?

Can you check if aardvark is running (`ps aux | grep aardvark`)? I think given that the nameserver in your output is 8.8.8.8 it will try to use aardvark but this fails so it falls back to 8.8.8.8 after the 5s timeout.

Thanks for the quick replies! 🙌

Host `/etc/resolv.conf` points to google dns:
```
# cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 8.8.8.8 8.8.4.4
```

Container `/etc/resolv.conf` looks like this:
```
# cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 10.89.1.1
nameserver 8.8.8.8
nameserver 8.8.4.4
```

aardvark is running:
```
# ps aux | grep aardvark
  744 root     /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns --config /run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns -p 53 run
22353 root     grep aardvark
```

That was very helpful, I think I found the issue now:

```
/ # time nslookup -debug google.com
Server:         10.89.1.1
Address:        10.89.1.1:53

Query #0 completed in 215ms:
Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 142.250.81.206

Query #1 completed in 229ms:
Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 2607:f8b0:4004:82f::200e

real    0m 0.25s
user    0m 0.00s
sys     0m 0.01s
```

The problem is the host `/etc/resolv.conf`. If I split the nameserver line into two lines, it works as expected. It did require a restart of aardvark-dns though to become effective.
Thanks so much for all the help!

Having to nameservers on the same line is not valid syntax according to resolv.conf(5) man page.

We might want to look into the parsing code in aardvark, I don't understand why it hangs more than 5s. If there are no valid entries we should just return and not hang.
Concur, this seems like an Aardvark bug - we should at least throw a warning that the host resolv.conf is not using valid syntax?
